agent_output: {"handoff_brief":{"recipient":"Shaq","task_context":"Implement a pure TypeScript function `recalculatePrice` that computes final price after applying bulk discounts. This function serves a high-traffic pricing endpoint (~500 req/sec peak). All three upstream agents (Bird, MJ, Pippen) are aligned on scope: a stateless, deterministic pure function with no external dependencies or I/O.","domain_rules":[{"rule":"Final price = base price minus applicable discounts, floored at $0.01. Price can never be zero or negative.","testable_assertion":"recalculatePrice(quantity, basePrice) >= 0.01 for all valid inputs"},{"rule":"Bulk discount of 10% applies when quantity >= 10.","testable_assertion":"recalculatePrice(10, 100) === 90 AND recalculatePrice(9, 100) === 100"}],"architecture_guidance":[{"decision":"Pure TypeScript function: recalculatePrice(quantity: number, basePrice: number): number","implementation_note":"Stateless, deterministic, no external dependencies. Inline execution is acceptable given no I/O."},{"decision":"Add JSDoc comment documenting the $0.01 floor behavior","implementation_note":"On-call engineers need to understand why a price of $0.01 can appear. Document the floor rule and the condition that triggers it directly above the function signature."}],"acceptance_criteria":[{"criterion":"Bulk discount applies at quantity threshold","given":"quantity=10, basePrice=$5.00","when":"recalculatePrice is called","then":"returns $4.50 (10% discount applied)"},{"criterion":"No discount below quantity threshold","given":"quantity=9, basePrice=$5.00","when":"recalculatePrice is called","then":"returns $5.00 (no discount)"},{"criterion":"Price floor enforced when discount would zero out price","given":"a discount that would reduce price to $0.00 or below","when":"recalculatePrice is called","then":"returns $0.01"},{"criterion":"Unit test suite covers all acceptance criteria","given":"the implemented function","when":"tests are run","then":"all three scenarios above pass, plus edge cases around the threshold and floor"}],"terminology_alignment":[{"domain_term":"bulk discount","technical_term":"10% multiplier applied when quantity >= 10","definition":"A percentage reduction (currently 10%) on base price, triggered when the order quantity meets or exceeds the bulk threshold (currently 10 units)"},{"domain_term":"price floor","technical_term":"Math.max(computedPrice, 0.01)","definition":"The minimum allowable final price ($0.01), enforced after all discounts are applied to prevent zero or negative prices"}],"contradictions_resolved":[],"open_questions":[]},"escalations":[],"confidence":{"level":95,"high_confidence_areas":["All three agents are fully aligned on scope, behavior, and constraints","Domain rules are unambiguous with concrete acceptance criteria","Architecture is trivial (pure function) with no integration concerns"],"low_confidence_areas":["Bulk discount percentage (10%) and threshold (10 units) are hardcoded — unclear if these should be configurable parameters for future flexibility"],"assumptions":["Inputs (quantity, basePrice) are assumed to be valid positive numbers; no input validation requirements were specified","Only one discount type (bulk) exists currently; no stacking or priority logic needed"]}}
